message:
fix(packs/cloud/aws): close safe-pattern bypass on describe-/list-/get- in arg values

The broad aws-describe / aws-list / aws-get safe patterns used
`aws\b.*?\s+\S+\s+(describe|list|get)-` — which matches any two
space-separated tokens followed by the read-only prefix. So a destructive
command whose arg value starts with `describe-`, `list-`, or `get-`
would be treated as read-only and bypass the destructive pattern:

  aws s3api delete-bucket --bucket prod --query describe-me
      -> matched aws-describe safe, skipped s3api-delete-bucket

  aws ec2 terminate-instances --instance-ids list-ids
      -> matched aws-list safe, skipped ec2-terminate

  aws iam delete-user --user-name get-creds-bot
      -> matched aws-get safe, skipped iam-delete

Fixed by swapping the `.*?` walker for `(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*` —
only flag-value pairs (`--?` tokens, optionally with a value) may
precede the service-name token. Positional args after the subcommand
can no longer pose as the service name.

Regression test verifies the three concrete bypass shapes are blocked
and that legitimate `aws ec2 describe-instances`, `aws s3api list-...`,
and `aws --profile prod ec2 describe-...` still pass.
